column1: # Real causal measurement of "does enabling layer_loop help at this point in training".
# At frac >= ENABLE_LOOPING_AT (0.35), the run snapshots state, probes K steps with
# loops OFF, restores, probes K steps with loops ON, restores, and commits the winner.
# Comparison: fork vs always at two seeds to check variance. 300-step runs to give
# the fork enough post-commit training budget (~200 steps after gate).
# Expected wallclock per run: ~45-55 min on Nano (300 training steps + ~30 probe steps + 2 probe evals).
#   - fork runs produce a decisions_*.md with A/B val_bpb and a chosen winner
#   - fork_seed1337 final train_loss is within 0.05 of always_seed1337 (proves no catastrophic rollback)
#   - across both seeds, fork's winner choice is consistent (either always-ON or always-OFF at this regime)
#     otherwise we've discovered data-dependent dynamics worth exploring further

column2: TIMEOUT_SECS=3600 MAX_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS=3000 QK_GAIN_INIT=5.5 WARMDOWN_FRAC=0.64 TTT_ENABLED=1 SLIDING_WINDOW_ENABLED=1 TTT_EPOCHS=1 EMA_DECAY=0.995 LOGIT_SOFTCAP=1000 SCALAR_LR=0.030 MATRIX_LR=0.040 TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS=32768 TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=1024 ITERATIONS=180 VAL_LOSS_EVERY=60 SEED=1337 LOOP_GATE_MODE=always DECISIONS_MD_PATH=logs/decisions_nano_s07_always_seed1337.md VAL_BATCH_TOKENS=8192 EVAL_SEQ_LEN=512 MAX_VAL_TOKENS=500000
